post.description:
Generates a GPO/SCCM-compatible configuration file for deploying the security product via centralized IT management systems (such as Group Policy or System Center Configuration Manager). 


The configuration is generated ad-hoc and not stored in the system.

get.description: List all installers available to the caller.

parameters.name: usable
parameters.description:
If true, only usable installers will be included in the response. If false, only unusable installers will be returned.

If empty, all installers will be included.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description:
Limit for pagination purposes.

If unspecified or 0, the default value is 50. The maximum value is 1000; values above 1000 will be coerced to 1000.

> **Info:**
> For more information, refer to [Paginating Requests in APIs](https://medium.com/@ignaciochiazzo/paginating-requests-in-apis-d4883d4c1c4c)
>
> or [Design Patterns: Pagination](https://google.aip.dev/158)

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description:
Page token of the current page.

If not given or "", the first page is returned.

> **Info:**
> For more information, refer to [Paginating Requests in APIs](https://medium.com/@ignaciochiazzo/paginating-requests-in-apis-d4883d4c1c4c)
>
> or [Design Patterns: Pagination](https://google.aip.dev/158)

subscriptionUuid.description:
Reference to a [subscription] from where the units will be used.

type: subscription_management.v1.Subscription

subscriptionSupportCode.description: User-friendly code used in support-related situations. Generated by the system at subscription creation, cannot be updated.

requestedComponentIds.description:
List of component IDs to be explicitly included in the installer. Each ID represents a platform component that provides specific security functionality. If not provided, components will be automatically selected based on the license.


Only the following component IDs are currently supported:

13 – ESET Remote Administrator Agent

Endpoint Security components:
19 - 	ESET Endpoint Antivirus for Windows
20 -  ESET File Security for Microsoft Windows Server
27 - 	ESET Endpoint Security for Windows

209 - ESET File Security for Linux
215 - ESET Endpoint Antivirus for Linux (new codebase, v7+)

29 - 	ESET Endpoint Antivirus for macOS
30 - 	ESET Endpoint Security for macOS
230 - ESET Endpoint Antivirus for macOS (new codebase, v7+)
231 - ESET Endpoint Security for macOS (new codebase, v7+)



167 – ESET Inspect Agent


Full Disk Encryption (EFDE) components:
210 - ESET Full Disk Encryption for Windows

Any unsupported or incompatible component ID will be rejected with a validation error.

type: PlatformComponent

groupDisplayName.description: Display name of the future parent device group.

securityGroupUuid.description:
Security group to become a member of. 

If not specified:
1. If the device name matches an existing device, the device is enrolled in the group of the matching device.
2. If the device name does not match any existing device, the device is enrolled in the `Lost & found` group.

type: asset_management.v1.Group

v1DeviceEnrollmentSettings.description: Describes how device enrolls into device management after installation.

sendAnonymousDiagnosticData.description: If true, the installed endpoint product will take part in improvement program by sending anonymous diagnostic data, such as crash reports and crash dumps.

gpoSccmFile.description: Base64-encoded contents of the generated file, ready for download or use.

activatedProducts.description: List of automatically determined [business products] to be activated (enabled) when the installation finishes.

createTime.description:
When the installer was created.

> **Info:**
> For details, see
> [GitHub: timestamp.proto](https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/blob/main/src/google/protobuf/timestamp.proto).

downloadUrl.description: The installer can be downloaded at this URL.

enableCloudScannerFeedback.description: If true, the installed endpoint product will use ESET cloud scanning.

enablePuaDetection.description: If true, the detection of potentially unwanted applications will be enabled in the installed endpoint product.

expireTime.description:
Installers are created with automatically determined lifespan. After expire_time, they become unusable.

> **Info:**
> For details, see
> [GitHub: timestamp.proto](https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/blob/main/src/google/protobuf/timestamp.proto).

installedComponents.description:
List of automatically determined [ESET platform component]s to be installed.

Every component can host one or more [business products] or feature sets (or capabilities or features). Activated (enabled) products are listed in the respective attribute.

operatingSystemFamilyId.description:
Which operating system family the installer supports. Currently, only 1 - Windows and 3 - MacOS are supported.

0 = Unspecified
1 = Windows
2 = Linux
3 = Mac OS

type: os_catalog.v2.OsFamily

preferredLanguageCode.description:
Code of the preferred GUI language in [BCP-47 format](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IETF_language_tag).

A default "en-us" is used if the preferred language is not available.

sendAnonymousDiagnosticData.description: If true, the installed endpoint product will take part in improvement program by sending anonymous diagnostic data, such as crash reports and crash dumps.

usable.description: If true, the installer can be used. If false, the installer cannot be used for various reasons (for example, expiration).

uuid.description:
Unique identifier of the entity.

Must be collision-free - two identifiers created anywhere in the world must not collide within entity parent scope. Unless a member of aggregate, the entity scope is always global.

Although most of the times compliant with [RFC 4122: A Universally Unique IDentifier (UUID) URN Namespace](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc4122), do not rely on it being a RFC UUID. Treat it as an opaque identifier.

RFC UUID can be recognized by being formatted according to the template xxxxxxxx-xxxx-Mxxx-Nxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx, as explained on [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Universally_unique_identifier#Format).

UUID is used for referencing an entity, even across domains.

Example: "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000"

v1Installer.description:
Describes the installer entity.

Installer:
1. drops one or more components on the target device(s)
2. activates one or more products on the target device(s)
3. enrolls the target device into device management under some security group.

installers.description: List of [installer]s matching criteria from the request.

nextPageToken.description:
Page token of the next page.

Empty or "" for the last page.

> **Info:**
> For more information, refer to [Paginating Requests in APIs](https://medium.com/@ignaciochiazzo/paginating-requests-in-apis-d4883d4c1c4c)
>
> or [Design Patterns: Pagination](https://google.aip.dev/158)

totalSize.description:
The total count of items in the list irrespective of pagination.

> **Info:**
> One of the [standard fields](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/standard_fields)

`Page_size` might differ for every call (it is an input parameter) so the calculation of how many pages there is in total is caller's responsibility.

displayName.description: Human-readable name of the component. Derived from package display_name.

id.description:
Identifier of the governed resource. Can be seen as a [value of item in enumerated type](https://protobuf.dev/programming-guides/proto3/#enum).

It can be derived from the name by some algorithm, such as CRC32.

name.description:
Name of the governed item. Can be seen as a name of an enumeration item, with `id` being its numerical value.

> **Note:**
> Not to be confused with `display_name`, which is [purposed for UI](https://google.aip.dev/148).

Bearer.description: Type "Bearer " and then your API Token
Bearer.name: Authorization
